title: aspidium acrostichoides
intro: Aspidium acrostichoides, also known as the Cinnamon fern, is a type of perennial fern native to eastern North America. It is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the Cinnamon fern can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water, and it is often used in teas and tinctures to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
